# Load the workbook using openpyxl to maintain formatting
wb = load_workbook(spreadsheet_path)
ws = wb.active

# Get all values from column B (starting from row 1)
column_b_values = []
for row in range(1, ws.max_row + 1):
    cell_value = ws[f'B{row}'].value
    if cell_value is not None:
        column_b_values.append(str(cell_value))

# Filter values that start with 'PK'
pk_values = []
for value in column_b_values:
    if value.startswith('PK'):
        pk_values.append(value)

# Fill the result in E4:E12 (9 cells total)
result_start_row = 4
result_end_row = 12
result_column = 'E'

# Clear the existing content in the target range first
for row in range(result_start_row, result_end_row + 1):
    ws[f'{result_column}{row}'].value = None

# Fill the filtered values into E4:E12
for i, pk_value in enumerate(pk_values):
    if i < (result_end_row - result_start_row + 1):  # Don't exceed the specified range
        ws[f'{result_column}{result_start_row + i}'].value = pk_value

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save(output_path)
print(f"Spreadsheet saved to: {output_path}")

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Load the Excel file
workbook = load_workbook('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/105-24/1_105-24_init.xlsx')

# Read the Productivity sheet to create ID-to-Name mapping
productivity_sheet = workbook['Productivity']

# Create ID to Name mapping dictionary
id_to_name = {}

# Read from row 3 onwards (since row 1 has headers, row 2 has "MUD ID" and "Name")
for row in range(3, productivity_sheet.max_row + 1):
    id_cell = productivity_sheet.cell(row=row, column=1).value  # Column A
    name_cell = productivity_sheet.cell(row=row, column=2).value  # Column B
    
    if id_cell and name_cell:
        id_to_name[str(id_cell)] = str(name_cell)

# Access the Open sheet
open_sheet = workbook['Open']

# Replace IDs in column C (starting from row 3, since row 2 has headers)
for row in range(3, open_sheet.max_row + 1):
    # Column C replacement
    cell_c = open_sheet.cell(row=row, column=3)  # Column C
    if cell_c.value and str(cell_c.value) in id_to_name:
        cell_c.value = id_to_name[str(cell_c.value)]
    
    # Column K replacement  
    cell_k = open_sheet.cell(row=row, column=11)  # Column K
    if cell_k.value and str(cell_k.value) in id_to_name:
        cell_k.value = id_to_name[str(cell_k.value)]

# Save the modified workbook
workbook.save('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_105-24_output.xlsx')

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Load the spreadsheet
spreadsheet_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/108-24/1_108-24_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_108-24_output.xlsx"

# Load the workbook
wb = load_workbook(spreadsheet_path)

# Read the BZTB sheet into a DataFrame
bztb_df = pd.read_excel(spreadsheet_path, sheet_name='BZTB')

# Filter the data based on the criteria:
# 1. Column B starts with 'COS Pass', 'COS Comm', or 'COS W/S'
# 2. Column B contains 'Used'
# 3. Exclude rows where column A is '398726'

# First, let's ensure we have the right column names - looking at the data structure
# The data shows columns without headers, so we need to handle this properly
if bztb_df.columns[0] == 'Unnamed: 0':  # If pandas created default column names
    bztb_df.columns = ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']  # Rename to match Excel columns
else:
    # Reset index to make sure we capture all data including the first row
    bztb_df = pd.read_excel(spreadsheet_path, sheet_name='BZTB', header=None)
    bztb_df.columns = ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']

# Apply the filtering criteria
filtered_df = bztb_df[
    (
        (bztb_df['B'].astype(str).str.startswith('COS Pass')) |
        (bztb_df['B'].astype(str).str.startswith('COS Comm')) |
        (bztb_df['B'].astype(str).str.startswith('COS W/S'))
    ) &
    (bztb_df['B'].astype(str).str.contains('Used', na=False)) &
    (bztb_df['A'] != 398726)  # Exclude 398726
]

# Select only columns A to D
result_df = filtered_df[['A', 'B', 'C', 'D']].copy()

# Create a new workbook or modify the existing one
# We'll work with the existing workbook structure
with pd.ExcelWriter(output_path, engine='openpyxl') as writer:
    # Copy all existing sheets first
    for sheet_name in wb.sheetnames:
        if sheet_name != 'Tema UV COS':
            df_temp = pd.read_excel(spreadsheet_path, sheet_name=sheet_name, header=None)
            df_temp.to_excel(writer, sheet_name=sheet_name, index=False, header=False)
    
    # Write the filtered data to 'Tema UV COS' sheet starting from A1
    result_df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name='Tema UV COS', index=False, header=False, startrow=0, startcol=0)

print(f"Filtered data has been written to {output_path}")
print(f"Number of rows extracted: {len(result_df)}")
print("Filtered data preview:")
print(result_df)

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Load the spreadsheet
file_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/109-21/1_109-21_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_109-21_output.xlsx"

# Load workbook with openpyxl to preserve formatting
wb = load_workbook(file_path)
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Read data starting from row 5 (index 4 in pandas, row 5 in Excel)
# First, let's identify all rows with data
data_rows = []
for row_idx in range(5, ws.max_row + 1):  # Starting from row 5
    # Check if row has any data in columns A:X
    has_data = False
    for col_idx in range(1, 25):  # Columns A to X
        cell_value = ws.cell(row=row_idx, column=col_idx).value
        if cell_value is not None and str(cell_value).strip() != '':
            has_data = True
            break
    
    if has_data:
        # Get the point total from column K (column 11)
        point_total = ws.cell(row=row_idx, column=11).value
        data_rows.append({
            'row_number': row_idx,
            'points': point_total if point_total is not None else 0
        })

# Group consecutive rows (groups are separated by empty rows)
groups = []
current_group = []

for i, row_data in enumerate(data_rows):
    if i == 0:
        current_group = [row_data]
    else:
        # Check if current row is consecutive to previous row
        prev_row = data_rows[i-1]['row_number']
        curr_row = row_data['row_number']
        
        if curr_row == prev_row + 1:
            # Consecutive row, add to current group
            current_group.append(row_data)
        else:
            # Gap found, start new group
            if current_group:
                groups.append(current_group)
            current_group = [row_data]
    
    # Add the last group
    if i == len(data_rows) - 1:
        if current_group:
            groups.append(current_group)

# Process each group
for group in groups:
    if not group:
        continue
    
    # Find the maximum points in the group
    max_points = max(row['points'] for row in group if isinstance(row['points'], (int, float)))
    
    # Mark each person in the group
    for row_data in group:
        row_num = row_data['row_number']
        points = row_data['points']
        
        # Determine if this person gets 'Y' or 'N'
        if len(group) == 1:  # Alone in group
            result = 'Y'
        elif isinstance(points, (int, float)) and points == max_points:  # Tied for highest
            result = 'Y'
        else:
            result = 'N'
        
        # Write to column Z (column 26)
        ws.cell(row=row_num, column=26).value = result

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save(output_path)

import pandas as pd
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import re

# Load the spreadsheet
file_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/118-50/1_118-50_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_118-50_output.xlsx"

# Read the Excel file
wb = load_workbook(file_path)
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Get all words from column A, filtering out None/empty values
words = []
for row in range(1, min(5001, ws.max_row + 1)):  # Check up to 5000 rows
    cell_value = ws.cell(row=row, column=1).value
    if cell_value and isinstance(cell_value, str):
        words.append(cell_value.strip().upper())

# Remove duplicates and sort alphabetically
words = sorted(list(set(words)))

# Clear column A and fill with sorted words
for row in range(1, 5001):
    ws.cell(row=row, column=1).value = None

for i, word in enumerate(words):
    if i < 5000:
        ws.cell(row=i+1, column=1).value = word

# Create a set for faster lookup
word_set = set(words)

# Find transformation pairs
def transform_word(word):
    """Transform word by moving 5th letter to front if word has 5+ letters"""
    if len(word) < 5:
        return None
    return word[4] + word[:4] + word[5:]

# Find all valid transformation pairs
pairs = []
for word in words:
    if len(word) >= 5:
        transformed = transform_word(word)
        if transformed and transformed in word_set and transformed != word:
            pairs.append((transformed, word))

# Group by endings in specified order
ending_order = ['ING', 'ERS', 'ATE', 'EST', 'ONE', 'IER', 'ILY']
grouped_pairs = {ending: [] for ending in ending_order}
other_pairs = []

for transformed, original in pairs:
    categorized = False
    for ending in ending_order:
        if original.endswith(ending):
            grouped_pairs[ending].append((transformed, original))
            categorized = True
            break
    if not categorized:
        other_pairs.append((transformed, original))

# Sort pairs within each group
for ending in ending_order:
    grouped_pairs[ending].sort()
other_pairs.sort()

# Clear columns C and D
for row in range(2, 5001):
    ws.cell(row=row, column=3).value = None
    ws.cell(row=row, column=4).value = None

# Fill results starting from row 2
current_row = 2
for ending in ending_order:
    for transformed, original in grouped_pairs[ending]:
        if current_row <= 5000:
            ws.cell(row=current_row, column=3).value = transformed
            ws.cell(row=current_row, column=4).value = original
            current_row += 1

# Add other pairs
for transformed, original in other_pairs:
    if current_row <= 5000:
        ws.cell(row=current_row, column=3).value = transformed
        ws.cell(row=current_row, column=4).value = original
        current_row += 1

# Save the workbook
wb.save(output_path)
print(f"Spreadsheet saved to {output_path}")

import pandas as pd

wb = load_workbook('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/11842/1_11842_init.xlsx')

# Get the Total sheet
total_sheet = wb['Total']

# Set up the dropdown value in O3 with a default employee name
total_sheet['O3'] = "PETER NORONHA"  # Default value

# Set up headers and labels for the summary table
months = ['JAN', 'FEB', 'MAR', 'APR', 'MAY', 'JUN', 'JUL', 'AUG', 'SEP', 'OCT', 'NOV', 'DEC']
categories = ['P', 'ODK', 'A', 'L', 'WO', 'PH', 'CO', 'ML']

# Set up the month headers in row 4 (N4 to Y4)
for col_idx, month in enumerate(months):
    total_sheet.cell(row=4, column=14+col_idx, value=month)  # Start from column N (14)

# Set up category labels in column M (M5 to M12)
category_labels = ['PRESENT', 'WORK AT KARAI', 'ABSENT', 'LEAVE', 'WEEKLY OFF', 'PUBLIC HOLIDAY', 'COMP. OFF', 'MEDICAL. LEAVE']
for i, label in enumerate(category_labels):
    total_sheet.cell(row=5+i, column=13, value=label)  # Column M

# Update the name display in O2 to reference O3
total_sheet['O2'] = '=$O$3'

# Month sheet names
month_sheets = ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May', 'Jun', 'Jul', 'Aug', 'Sep', 'Oct', 'Nov', 'Dec']

# Create the formulas for the attendance summary (N5:Y12)
for row_idx, category in enumerate(categories):
    for col_idx, month_sheet in enumerate(month_sheets):
        # Calculate the cell position
        target_row = 5 + row_idx  # Start from row 5
        target_col = 14 + col_idx  # Start from column N (14)
        
        # Map categories to their column positions in monthly sheets
        # Based on the structure: P=32, ODK=33, A=34, L=35, WO=36, PH=37, CO=38, ML=39
        category_col_map = {'P': 'AG', 'ODK': 'AH', 'A': 'AI', 'L': 'AJ', 'WO': 'AK', 'PH': 'AL', 'CO': 'AM', 'ML': 'AN'}
        category_col_letter = category_col_map[category]
        
        # Create SUMPRODUCT formula to sum values where name matches the selection in O3
        # This accounts for multiple rows per person (first and second half of workday)
        formula = f'=SUMPRODUCT(({month_sheet}!A:A=$O$3)*{month_sheet}!{category_col_letter}:{category_col_letter})'
        
        total_sheet.cell(row=target_row, column=target_col, value=formula)

# Also fill the range O5:Z12 as specified in answer_position
# This extends a bit further than needed but covers the specified range
for row in range(5, 13):  # Rows 5-12
    for col in range(15, 27):  # Columns O-Z (15-26)
        if col <= 25:  # Only up to column Y since we have 12 months
            # These cells already have formulas from above
            pass
        else:
            # Fill extra columns with 0 or leave empty
            total_sheet.cell(row=row, column=col, value=0)

# Save the workbook
wb.save('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_11842_output.xlsx')

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl.styles import PatternFill

# Load the workbook
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/120-24/1_120-24_init.xlsx')
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Define the blue fill color
blue_fill = PatternFill(start_color='0070C0', end_color='0070C0', fill_type='solid')

# Convert column letters to numbers for easier processing
def col_letter_to_num(letter):
    result = 0
    for char in letter:
        result = result * 26 + ord(char) - ord('A') + 1
    return result

def col_num_to_letter(num):
    result = ""
    while num > 0:
        num -= 1
        result = chr(num % 26 + ord('A')) + result
        num //= 26
    return result

# Column mappings (AY=51, BG=59, BL=64, BN=66)

import pandas as pd
from openpyxl import load_workbook
from datetime import datetime

# Load the spreadsheet
spreadsheet_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/13-1/1_13-1_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_13-1_output.xlsx"

# Load workbook and sheets
wb = load_workbook(spreadsheet_path)
ranges_sheet = wb['RANGES']
lists_sheet = wb['LISTS']

# Read RANGES data, skipping header and empty rows
ranges_data = []
for row in ranges_sheet.iter_rows(min_row=2, values_only=True):  # Skip header
    if row and row[1] is not None and str(row[1]).strip() != '' and str(row[1]).upper() != 'DATE':
        ranges_data.append({
            'SN': row[0],
            'DATE': row[1],
            'REF': row[3],  # REF is in column D (index 3)
            'AMOUNTS': row[4]  # AMOUNTS is in column E (index 4)
        })

# Convert to DataFrame for easier manipulation
df_ranges = pd.DataFrame(ranges_data)

# Process only if we have data
if not df_ranges.empty:
    # Handle date conversion more carefully
    def parse_date(date_val):
        if isinstance(date_val, datetime):
            return date_val
        elif isinstance(date_val, str):
            try:
                return pd.to_datetime(date_val, format='%m/%d/%Y')
            except:
                try:
                    return pd.to_datetime(date_val)
                except:
                    return date_val
        else:
            return date_val
    
    df_ranges['DATE'] = df_ranges['DATE'].apply(parse_date)
    
    # Group by DATE and REF, sum the amounts
    grouped = df_ranges.groupby(['DATE', 'REF']).agg({
        'AMOUNTS': 'sum'
    }).reset_index()
    
    # Sort by DATE (ascending) then REF (alphabetically)
    grouped = grouped.sort_values(['DATE', 'REF'])
    
    # Create the final data structure with TOTAL rows
    grouped_data = []
    current_sn = 1
    current_date = None
    date_group_amounts = []
    
    for _, row in grouped.iterrows():
        # If we're starting a new date group and we have previous data, add TOTAL row
        if current_date is not None and current_date != row['DATE']:
            grouped_data.append({
                'SN': '',
                'DATE': '',
                'REF': 'TOTAL',
                'AMOUNTS': sum(date_group_amounts)
            })
            date_group_amounts = []
        
        # Add current row
        grouped_data.append({
            'SN': current_sn,
            'DATE': row['DATE'],
            'REF': row['REF'],
            'AMOUNTS': row['AMOUNTS']
        })
        
        current_sn += 1
        current_date = row['DATE']
        date_group_amounts.append(row['AMOUNTS'])
    
    # Add final TOTAL row
    if date_group_amounts:
        grouped_data.append({
            'SN': '',
            'DATE': '',
            'REF': 'TOTAL',
            'AMOUNTS': sum(date_group_amounts)
        })
else:
    grouped_data = []

# Clear the old data in LISTS sheet (keep headers in row 1)
# Clear from A3 to D32 as specified in answer_position
for row in lists_sheet['A3:D32']:
    for cell in row:
        cell.value = None

# Write the new data starting from row 3 (A3:D32 range)
start_row = 3
for i, data_row in enumerate(grouped_data):
    if start_row + i > 32:  # Don't exceed the specified range
        break
    
    row_num = start_row + i
    lists_sheet[f'A{row_num}'] = data_row['SN']
    lists_sheet[f'B{row_num}'] = data_row['DATE']
    lists_sheet[f'C{row_num}'] = data_row['REF']
    lists_sheet[f'D{row_num}'] = data_row['AMOUNTS']

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save(output_path)

import pandas as pd
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import os

def solve_spreadsheet_lookup():
    # Input and output paths
    input_path = '/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/13284/1_13284_init.xlsx'
    output_path = '/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_13284_output.xlsx'
    
    # Create output directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(output_path), exist_ok=True)
    
    try:
        # Load the workbook
        wb = load_workbook(input_path)
        
        # Get the sheets
        base_sheet = wb['Base']
        streets_sheet = wb['Streets']
        
        # Read data from Streets sheet into a list for easier processing
        streets_data = []
        for row in streets_sheet.iter_rows(min_row=2, values_only=True):
            if row[1] is not None:  # Check if column B (index 1) has a value
                streets_data.append({
                    'B': row[1],  # Column B
                    'C': row[2],  # Column C (Start)
                    'D': row[3],  # Column D (End)
                    'F': row[5]   # Column F (Name to return)
                })
        
        # Process each row in Base sheet from E2 to E26
        for row_num in range(2, 27):  # Rows 2 to 26
            base_c_value = base_sheet[f'C{row_num}'].value
            base_d_value = base_sheet[f'D{row_num}'].value
            
            # Initialize result as empty string
            result = ""
            
            # Skip if either C or D is empty in Base sheet
            if base_c_value is None or base_d_value is None:
                base_sheet[f'E{row_num}'] = result
                continue
            
            # Look for matches in Streets data
            for street_row in streets_data:
                # Check first condition: Base!C matches Streets!B
                if base_c_value == street_row['B']:
                    # Check second condition: Base!D falls within Streets!C and Streets!D range
                    # Only consider if both Start (C) and End (D) are populated
                    if (street_row['C'] is not None and 
                        street_row['D'] is not None):
                        
                        try:
                            # Convert to numbers for range comparison
                            base_d_num = float(base_d_value)
                            start_num = float(street_row['C'])
                            end_num = float(street_row['D'])
                            
                            # Check if base_d_num falls within the range [start_num, end_num]
                            if start_num <= base_d_num <= end_num:
                                result = street_row['F'] if street_row['F'] is not None else ""
                                break
                                
                        except (ValueError, TypeError):
                            # Skip if conversion to float fails
                            continue
            
            # Set the result in column E
            base_sheet[f'E{row_num}'] = result
        
        # Save the modified workbook
        wb.save(output_path)
        print(f"Successfully saved the modified spreadsheet to: {output_path}")
        
    except FileNotFoundError:
        print(f"Error: Input file not found at {input_path}")
        # Create a minimal workbook for testing purposes
        from openpyxl import Workbook
        wb = Workbook()
        wb.save(output_path)
        print(f"Created empty workbook at {output_path}")
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error processing spreadsheet: {str(e)}")
        # Create a minimal workbook as fallback
        from openpyxl import Workbook
        wb = Workbook()
        wb.save(output_path)
        print(f"Created fallback workbook at {output_path}")

# Execute the function
solve_spreadsheet_lookup()

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl.styles import PatternFill

# Load the Excel file
file_path = '/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/141-20/1_141-20_init.xlsx'
output_path = '/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_141-20_output.xlsx'

# Read both sheets
pl_recon_df = pd.read_excel(file_path, sheet_name='PL Recon Items')
statement_recon_df = pd.read_excel(file_path, sheet_name='Statement Recon Items')

print("Original PL Recon Items:")
print(pl_recon_df)
print("\nOriginal Statement Recon Items:")
print(statement_recon_df)

# Find matching rows
# Column C (index 2) in PL Recon Items = Invoice No.
# Column D (index 3) in PL Recon Items = Amount
# Column F (index 5) in Statement Recon Items = Reference
# Column I (index 8) in Statement Recon Items = Total Invoice Value

rows_to_delete_pl = []
rows_to_delete_statement = []

# Check for matches
for pl_idx, pl_row in pl_recon_df.iterrows():
    invoice_no = pl_row['Invoice No.']
    amount = pl_row['Amount']
    
    for stmt_idx, stmt_row in statement_recon_df.iterrows():
        reference = stmt_row['Reference']
        total_value = stmt_row['Total Invoice Value']
        
        # Check if invoice number and amount match
        if invoice_no == reference and amount == total_value:
            rows_to_delete_pl.append(pl_idx)
            rows_to_delete_statement.append(stmt_idx)
            print(f"Match found: PL row {pl_idx} (Invoice: {invoice_no}, Amount: {amount}) matches Statement row {stmt_idx}")

# Remove duplicates and sort in reverse order to delete from bottom to top
rows_to_delete_pl = sorted(list(set(rows_to_delete_pl)), reverse=True)
rows_to_delete_statement = sorted(list(set(rows_to_delete_statement)), reverse=True)

print(f"\nRows to delete from PL Recon Items: {rows_to_delete_pl}")
print(f"Rows to delete from Statement Recon Items: {rows_to_delete_statement}")

# Delete matching rows from both dataframes
pl_recon_filtered = pl_recon_df.drop(rows_to_delete_pl)
statement_recon_filtered = statement_recon_df.drop(rows_to_delete_statement)

print("\nFiltered PL Recon Items:")
print(pl_recon_filtered)
print("\nFiltered Statement Recon Items:")
print(statement_recon_filtered)

# Create a new Excel file with the filtered data
with pd.ExcelWriter(output_path, engine='openpyxl') as writer:
    pl_recon_filtered.to_excel(writer, sheet_name='PL Recon Items', index=False)
    statement_recon_filtered.to_excel(writer, sheet_name='Statement Recon Items', index=False)

print(f"\nModified Excel file saved to: {output_path}")

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Load the spreadsheet
input_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/142-19/1_142-19_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_142-19_output.xlsx"

# Load the workbook and worksheet
wb = load_workbook(input_path)
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Read data from the specified range A1:B82
data = []
for row in range(1, 83):  # A1:B82 means rows 1-82
    cell_a = ws[f'A{row}'].value
    cell_b = ws[f'B{row}'].value
    data.append([cell_a, cell_b])

# Function to identify non-blank ranges
def find_non_blank_ranges(data):
    ranges = []
    start = None
    
    for i, (col_a, col_b) in enumerate(data):
        # Check if both columns are blank/None
        is_blank = (col_a is None or str(col_a).strip() == '') and (col_b is None or str(col_b).strip() == '')
        
        if not is_blank:
            if start is None:
                start = i
        else:
            if start is not None:
                ranges.append((start, i - 1))
                start = None
    
    # Handle case where data ends with a non-blank range
    if start is not None:
        ranges.append((start, len(data) - 1))
    
    return ranges

# Find non-blank ranges
ranges = find_non_blank_ranges(data)

# Sort each range by Column A
for start_idx, end_idx in ranges:
    # Extract the range data

    for row in range_data:
        col_a, col_b = row
        if not ((col_a is None or str(col_a).strip() == '') and (col_b is None or str(col_b).strip() == '')):
            non_blank_rows.append(row)
    
    # Sort by Column A (convert to string for consistent sorting)
    non_blank_rows.sort(key=lambda x: str(x[0]) if x[0] is not None else '')
    
    # Replace the original range with sorted data
    for i, sorted_row in enumerate(non_blank_rows):
        data[start_idx + i] = sorted_row
    
    # Clear any remaining rows in the range if sorted data is shorter
    for i in range(len(non_blank_rows), end_idx - start_idx + 1):
        data[start_idx + i] = [None, None]

# Write the sorted data back to the worksheet
for row_idx, (col_a, col_b) in enumerate(data, 1):
    ws[f'A{row_idx}'].value = col_a
    ws[f'B{row_idx}'].value = col_b

# Save the workbook
wb.save(output_path)
print(f"Spreadsheet saved to {output_path}")

            # Replace all S with the same vowel

            if word2 in words and word1 != word2:
                # Make sure we don't duplicate pairs (order them consistently)
                pair = tuple(sorted([word1, word2]))
                if pair not in pairs:
                    pairs.append(pair)

# Clear columns G and H in the specified range

    month_pattern = re.findall(r'_(\d{2})_', str(a3_value))
    if month_pattern:

    else:
        # Try single digit patterns

# Set up input cells B1:F1 with initial values (can be changed by user)
ws['B1'] = 0
ws['C1'] = 1
ws['D1'] = 1
ws['E1'] = 0
ws['F1'] = 1

# Based on the spreadsheet content, it appears we have customers A, B, C, D, E
# in row 2 (columns B through F)
# We need to create formulas in A13 and A14 that will return customer names 
# when the corresponding input in row 1 equals 0

# For A13: Use INDEX and MATCH to find the first customer where the input is 0
# We'll use a combination of INDEX, MATCH, and SMALL functions
ws['A13'] = '=INDEX($B$2:$F$2,MATCH(SMALL(IF($B$1:$F$1=0,COLUMN($B$1:$F$1)),1),COLUMN($B$1:$F$1),0))'

# For A14: Find the second customer where the input is 0
ws['A14'] = '=IFERROR(INDEX($B$2:$F$2,MATCH(SMALL(IF($B$1:$F$1=0,COLUMN($B$1:$F$1)),2),COLUMN($B$1:$F$1),0)),"")'

# Since Excel array formulas might not work as expected, let's use a simpler approach
# Let's create formulas that concatenate all customers where the input is 0

# Alternative approach using nested IF statements for A13 (first match)
formula_a13 = '=IF(B1=0,B2,IF(C1=0,C2,IF(D1=0,D2,IF(E1=0,E2,IF(F1=0,F2,"")))))'
ws['A13'] = formula_a13

# For A14 (second match), we need to find the next occurrence
# This is more complex, so let's use a different approach
formula_a14 = '''=IF(AND(B1=0,C1=0),C2,IF(AND(B1=0,D1=0,C1<>0),D2,IF(AND(B1=0,E1=0,C1<>0,D1<>0),E2,IF(AND(B1=0,F1=0,C1<>0,D1<>0,E1<>0),F2,IF(AND(C1=0,D1=0,B1<>0),D2,IF(AND(C1=0,E1=0,B1<>0,D1<>0),E2,IF(AND(C1=0,F1=0,B1<>0,D1<>0,E1<>0),F2,IF(AND(D1=0,E1=0,B1<>0,C1<>0),E2,IF(AND(D1=0,F1=0,B1<>0,C1<>0,E1<>0),F2,IF(AND(E1=0,F1=0,B1<>0,C1<>0,D1<>0),F2,""))))))))))'''

# The above formula is too complex. Let's use a simpler approach with helper logic
# For A14, let's find the second customer with 0
ws['A14'] = '=IF(COUNTIF($B$1:$F$1,0)>1,INDEX($B$2:$F$2,MATCH(0,IF(COLUMN($B$1:$F$1)>MATCH(A13,$B$2:$F$2,0)+1,$B$1:$F$1,1),0)),"")'

# Actually, let's use a much simpler and more reliable approach
# Using SMALL and COLUMN functions to find positions of zeros

# Clear previous formulas and use a more straightforward approach
ws['A13'] = None
ws['A14'] = None

# Create helper area to identify positions of zeros
# We'll put a more practical solution:

# For A13 - First customer with 0 in input row
ws['A13'] = '=IF(B1=0,B2,IF(C1=0,C2,IF(D1=0,D2,IF(E1=0,E2,IF(F1=0,F2,"")))))'

# For A14 - Second customer with 0 in input row (skip the first found)

# Load the workbook and get the active sheet
wb = load_workbook(spreadsheet_path)
ws = wb.active

# Get the range we need to work with (B2:B30, which is rows 2-30 in Excel, 1-29 in 0-indexed)
# First, let's copy values from column A to column B for the header and initial setup
for row in range(1, 31):  # rows 1-30 in Excel (0-29 in 0-indexed)
    # Get value from column A
    cell_a_value = ws[f'A{row}'].value
    
    # If we're in the range B2:B30 (rows 2-30), we need to fill blanks
    if row >= 2:
        # If column A has a value, use it; otherwise keep the previous value from column B
        if cell_a_value is not None and str(cell_a_value).strip() != '':
            ws[f'B{row}'] = cell_a_value
        else:
            # Get the last non-blank value from column B above current row
            last_value = None
            for prev_row in range(row-1, 0, -1):  # Go backwards from current row
                prev_b_value = ws[f'B{prev_row}'].value
                if prev_b_value is not None and str(prev_b_value).strip() != '':
                    last_value = prev_b_value
                    break
                # Also check column A for the original values
                prev_a_value = ws[f'A{prev_row}'].value
                if prev_a_value is not None and str(prev_a_value).strip() != '':
                    last_value = prev_a_value
                    break
            
            # Fill with the last found value
            if last_value is not None:
                ws[f'B{row}'] = last_value

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save(output_path)

import pandas as pd

from openpyxl import load_workbook

def delete_empty_rows():
    # Load the workbook
    workbook = load_workbook('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/157-4/1_157-4_init.xlsx')
    
    # Get Sheet1
    worksheet = workbook['Sheet1']
    
    # We need to delete rows from bottom to top to avoid index shifting issues
    # Check rows 14 to 83 (1-based indexing in Excel)
    rows_to_delete = []
    
    for row_num in range(14, 84):  # 14 to 83 inclusive
        # Check columns F to L (6 to 12 in 1-based indexing, 5 to 11 in 0-based)
        # In openpyxl, we use 1-based indexing
        row_is_empty = True
        
        # Check columns F(6) through L(12)
        for col_num in range(6, 13):  # F=6, G=7, H=8, I=9, J=10, K=11, L=12
            cell_value = worksheet.cell(row=row_num, column=col_num).value
            if cell_value is not None and str(cell_value).strip() != "":
                row_is_empty = False
                break
        
        if row_is_empty:
            rows_to_delete.append(row_num)
    
    # Delete rows from bottom to top to avoid index shifting
    for row_num in sorted(rows_to_delete, reverse=True):
        worksheet.delete_rows(row_num, 1)
    
    # Save the modified workbook
    workbook.save('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_157-4_output.xlsx')

delete_empty_rows()

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import numpy as np

# Load the spreadsheet
input_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/160-6/1_160-6_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_160-6_output.xlsx"

# Load the workbook and get the SH sheet
workbook = load_workbook(input_path)
sh_sheet = workbook['SH']

# Read all data from the sheet into a list of lists
data = []
max_row = sh_sheet.max_row
max_col = sh_sheet.max_column

# Get header row (assuming first row contains headers)
headers = []
for col in range(1, max_col + 1):
    cell_value = sh_sheet.cell(row=1, column=col).value
    headers.append(cell_value)

# Get all data rows
for row in range(1, max_row + 1):
    row_data = []
    for col in range(1, max_col + 1):
        cell_value = sh_sheet.cell(row=row, column=col).value
        row_data.append(cell_value)
    data.append(row_data)

# Filter out blank rows (rows where all cells are None or empty)
filtered_data = []
for row_data in data:
    # Check if row is completely blank
    is_blank = all(cell is None or cell == '' or (isinstance(cell, float) and pd.isna(cell)) for cell in row_data)
    if not is_blank:
        filtered_data.append(row_data)

# Clear the existing sheet content
for row in sh_sheet.iter_rows():
    for cell in row:
        cell.value = None

# Write the filtered data back to the sheet
for row_idx, row_data in enumerate(filtered_data, 1):
    for col_idx, cell_value in enumerate(row_data, 1):
        sh_sheet.cell(row=row_idx, column=col_idx, value=cell_value)

# Save the workbook to the output path
workbook.save(output_path)

# The answer_position is F2:F10, which corresponds to the Track No column
# We need to create a formula that increments track numbers for each album

# For Excel formulas, we need to create a COUNTIF formula that counts how many times
# the current album name appears up to the current row, which will give us the track number

# Starting from row 2 (F2) to row 10 (F10)
for row in range(2, 11):  # rows 2-10 in Excel (1-indexed)
    # The formula will count occurrences of the album name (column D) up to current row
    # COUNTIF($D$2:D{row}, D{row}) will count how many times the current album appears
    # from the beginning up to the current row
    formula = f'=COUNTIF($D$2:D{row},D{row})'
    ws[f'F{row}'] = formula

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save(output_path)
print(f"Spreadsheet saved with track number formulas to: {output_path}")

df_sheet2 = pd.read_excel(spreadsheet_path, sheet_name='Sheet2')
df_sheet3 = pd.read_excel(spreadsheet_path, sheet_name='Sheet3')

# Get the headers from Sheet2 (column names)
sheet2_headers = df_sheet2.columns.tolist()

# Get entries from Sheet1
sheet1_entries = df_sheet1['Entry'].dropna().tolist()

# Create a list to store all output combinations
output_combinations = []

# For each header in Sheet2
for header in sheet2_headers:
    # Check if this header exists in any Sheet1 entry
    matching_entries = []
    for entry in sheet1_entries:
        if header in entry:
            matching_entries.append(entry)
    
    # If we found matching entries
    if matching_entries:
        # Get all non-null values from this header's column in Sheet2
        sheet2_values = df_sheet2[header].dropna().tolist()
        
        # For each matching entry in Sheet1
        for entry in matching_entries:
            # For each value in Sheet2 under this header
            for value in sheet2_values:
                # Create combination: entry + value
                combination = f"{entry}{value}"
                output_combinations.append(combination)

# Create new Sheet3 with all combinations
df_sheet3_new = pd.DataFrame({'Output': output_combinations})

# Load the workbook and update sheets
with pd.ExcelWriter(output_path, engine='openpyxl') as writer:
    # Write all sheets
    df_sheet1.to_excel(writer, sheet_name='Sheet1', index=False)
    df_sheet2.to_excel(writer, sheet_name='Sheet2', index=False)
    df_sheet3_new.to_excel(writer, sheet_name='Sheet3', index=False)

print(f"Successfully created output file with {len(output_combinations)} combinations in Sheet3")

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from openpyxl import load_workbook
from openpyxl.styles import NamedStyle
from openpyxl.utils.dataframe import dataframe_to_rows

# Load the spreadsheet
file_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/177-6/1_177-6_init.xlsx"

# Group by Column H (ComboKey) and process the data
combo_key_col = df.columns[7]  # Column H (0-indexed as 7)

# Group the data by ComboKey
grouped = df.groupby(combo_key_col)

# Create a list to store the merged rows
merged_rows = []

for combo_key, group in grouped:
    # Take the first row as the base (columns A:H should be identical)
    merged_row = group.iloc[0].copy()
    
    # Sum the numerical columns (I:R, which are columns 8:17 in 0-indexed)
    # First, identify which columns are numerical in the range I:R
    numeric_cols = []
    for i in range(8, min(18, len(df.columns))):  # Columns I:R (8:17)
        if i < len(df.columns):
            numeric_cols.append(df.columns[i])
    
    # Sum the numerical columns for this group
    for col in numeric_cols:
        # Convert to numeric, replacing non-numeric values with 0
        numeric_series = pd.to_numeric(group[col], errors='coerce').fillna(0)
        merged_row[col] = numeric_series.sum()
    
    merged_rows.append(merged_row)

# Create the merged dataframe
merged_df = pd.DataFrame(merged_rows)

# Load the workbook to preserve formatting and work with the combined sheet
wb = load_workbook(file_path)

# Clear the combined sheet if it exists
if 'combined' in wb.sheetnames:
    wb.remove(wb['combined'])

# Create new combined sheet
combined_sheet = wb.create_sheet('combined')

# Write headers
for c_idx, column_name in enumerate(merged_df.columns, 1):
    combined_sheet.cell(row=1, column=c_idx, value=column_name)

# Write data with special formatting for columns I:R
for r_idx, (_, row) in enumerate(merged_df.iterrows(), 2):
    for c_idx, (col_name, value) in enumerate(row.items(), 1):
        cell = combined_sheet.cell(row=r_idx, column=c_idx)
        
        # Check if this is in columns I:R (columns 9:18 in 1-indexed Excel)
        if c_idx >= 9 and c_idx <= 18:
            # Format numerical columns with 2 decimals, no commas, blanks for 0s
            if pd.isna(value) or value == 0:
                cell.value = ""
            else:
                cell.value = float(value)
                cell.number_format = '0.00'
        else:
            # For other columns, use the original value
            cell.value = value

# Save the workbook
wb.save(output_path)
print(f"Merged data saved to {output_path}")

result_df['Contact no'] = result_df['Contact no'].astype('object')
result_df['Mail id'] = result_df['Mail id'].astype('object')

# Process each location in the result sheet
for idx, row in result_df.iterrows():
    location = row['Location']
    
    # Find matching employee in main sheet for this location
    matching_employee = main_df[main_df['Location'] == location]
    
    if not matching_employee.empty:
        employee = matching_employee.iloc[0]  # Take first match if multiple
        designation = employee['Designation']
        
        # Check if designation matches criteria
        if designation in target_designations:
            result_df.at[idx, 'Designation'] = designation
            result_df.at[idx, 'Contact no'] = str(employee['Contact no'])
            result_df.at[idx, 'Mail id'] = employee['Mail id']

            result_df.at[idx, 'Designation'] = 'N/A'
            result_df.at[idx, 'Contact no'] = 'N/A'
            result_df.at[idx, 'Mail id'] = 'N/A'

    result_ws[f'B{row_num}'] = row['Designation']
    result_ws[f'C{row_num}'] = row['Contact no']
    result_ws[f'D{row_num}'] = row['Mail id']

    result_ws[f'C{row_num}'].alignment = Alignment(horizontal='left')
    result_ws[f'D{row_num}'].alignment = Alignment(horizontal='left')

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save(output_path)

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import os

# Load the spreadsheet to understand its structure
spreadsheet_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/18935/1_18935_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_18935_output.xlsx"

# Create output directory if it doesn't exist
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(output_path), exist_ok=True)

# Load the workbook
wb = load_workbook(spreadsheet_path)
ws = wb.active

# First, let's examine the full structure by reading all data
data = []
for row in ws.iter_rows(values_only=True):
    data.append(row)

# Print the structure to understand the layout
print("Spreadsheet structure:")
for i, row in enumerate(data[:25], 1):  # Print first 25 rows
    print(f"Row {i}: {row}")

# Find the data table - looking for Work, Material columns
data_table_row = None
for i, row in enumerate(data):
    if row and len(row) >= 3:
        # Check if this row contains the headers
        row_str = [str(cell).lower() if cell else '' for cell in row[:5]]
        if 'work' in row_str and 'material' in row_str:
            data_table_row = i + 1  # Convert to Excel row number (1-based)
            print(f"Found data table headers at row {data_table_row}")
            break

# Create lookup formulas for cells D17:D22
# Based on the structure, we need to create formulas that lookup from the data table

for row_num in range(17, 23):  # D17 to D22
    # Check what's in the current row to understand the lookup criteria
    work_value = ws[f'A{row_num}'].value
    material_value = ws[f'B{row_num}'].value
    category_type = ws[f'C{row_num}'].value
    
    print(f"Row {row_num}: Work={work_value}, Material={material_value}, Category Type={category_type}")
    
    # Create a lookup formula based on the data structure
    # Assuming data table starts at row 3 (as seen in the preview)
    # Work in column A, Material in column B, Categories in columns C, D, E
    
    # Use INDEX-MATCH with multiple criteria
    if category_type:
        if "1" in str(category_type) or "category 1" in str(category_type).lower():
            # Look up Category 1 (column C)
            formula = f'=INDEX(C$3:C$10,MATCH(1,IF(A$3:A$10=A{row_num},IF(B$3:B$10=B{row_num},1,0),0),0))'
        elif "2" in str(category_type) or "category 2" in str(category_type).lower():
            # Look up Category 2 (column D)  
            formula = f'=INDEX(D$3:D$10,MATCH(1,IF(A$3:A$10=A{row_num},IF(B$3:B$10=B{row_num},1,0),0),0))'
        else:
            # Look up Category 3 (column E)
            formula = f'=INDEX(E$3:E$10,MATCH(1,IF(A$3:A$10=A{row_num},IF(B$3:B$10=B{row_num},1,0),0),0),0))'
    else:
        # If no category type specified, use a more general approach
        # Use SUMPRODUCT for multiple criteria lookup
        formula = f'=IFERROR(INDEX(C$3:C$10,MATCH(1,(A$3:A$10=A{row_num})*(B$3:B$10=B{row_num}),0)),"")'
    
    # For better Excel compatibility, let's use SUMPRODUCT approach
    formula = f'=SUMPRODUCT((A$3:A$10=A{row_num})*(B$3:B$10=B{row_num})*C$3:C$10)'
    
    # If we need to select different category columns based on criteria, use nested IF
    if ws[f'C{row_num}'].value:
        category_ref = f'C{row_num}'
        formula = f'=IF(ISNUMBER(SEARCH("1",{category_ref})),SUMPRODUCT((A$3:A$10=A{row_num})*(B$3:B$10=B{row_num})*C$3:C$10),IF(ISNUMBER(SEARCH("2",{category_ref})),SUMPRODUCT((A$3:A$10=A{row_num})*(B$3:B$10=B{row_num})*D$3:D$10),SUMPRODUCT((A$3:A$10=A{row_num})*(B$3:B$10=B{row_num})*E$3:E$10)))'
    
    # Apply the formula to the cell
    ws[f'D{row_num}'] = formula
    print(f"Added formula to D{row_num}: {formula}")

# Save the modified workbook
try:
    wb.save(output_path)
    print(f"Successfully saved modified spreadsheet to: {output_path}")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error saving file: {e}")
    # Try saving to a simpler path as backup
    backup_path = "1_18935_output.xlsx"
    wb.save(backup_path)
    print(f"Saved backup file to: {backup_path}")

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl.styles import Font, Alignment
from openpyxl.utils.dataframe import dataframe_to_rows
import numpy as np

def solve_spreadsheet_manipulation():
    # Load the workbook
    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/191-40/1_191-40_init.xlsx')
    ws = wb['Sheet1']
    
    # Set calculation mode to automatic
    wb.calculation.calcMode = 'auto'
    
    # Get the data range (A1:H85)
    max_row = 85
    max_col = 8  # Column H
    
    # Read all data including formulas
    data = []
    for row in range(1, max_row + 1):
        row_data = []
        for col in range(1, max_col + 1):
            cell = ws.cell(row=row, column=col)
            if cell.value is None:
                row_data.append('')
            else:
                row_data.append(cell.value)
        data.append(row_data)
    
    # Identify blocks (separated by blank rows)
    blocks = []
    current_block = []
    current_block_start = 0
    
    for i, row_data in enumerate(data):
        # Check if row is blank (all cells are empty or contain only whitespace)
        is_blank = all(str(cell).strip() == '' or cell == '' for cell in row_data)
        
        if is_blank:
            if current_block:
                blocks.append({
                    'data': current_block,
                    'start_row': current_block_start,
                    'end_row': i - 1
                })
                current_block = []
        else:
            if not current_block:
                current_block_start = i
            current_block.append((i, row_data))
    
    # Add the last block if it exists
    if current_block:
        blocks.append({
            'data': current_block,
            'start_row': current_block_start,
            'end_row': len(data) - 1
        })
    
    # Sort each block by column D (index 3) in descending order
    sorted_data = [''] * len(data)  # Initialize with empty strings for blank rows
    
    for block in blocks:
        block_data = block['data']
        
        # Extract column D values for sorting
        sort_values = []
        for row_idx, row_data in block_data:
            cell = ws.cell(row=row_idx + 1, column=4)  # Column D
            
            # Get the evaluated value for sorting
            if cell.data_type == 'f':  # Formula
                # Force calculation and get numeric value
                try:
                    # For formulas, we need to evaluate them
                    wb.calculation.calcMode = 'auto'
                    numeric_value = float(cell.value) if cell.value is not None else 0
                except (ValueError, TypeError):
                    numeric_value = 0
            else:
                try:
                    numeric_value = float(cell.value) if cell.value is not None else 0
                except (ValueError, TypeError):
                    numeric_value = 0
            
            sort_values.append(numeric_value)
        
        # Sort by column D values in descending order
        sorted_indices = sorted(range(len(block_data)), key=lambda i: sort_values[i], reverse=True)
        
        # Place sorted data back
        for i, sorted_idx in enumerate(sorted_indices):
            original_row_idx, row_data = block_data[sorted_idx]

output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_192-22_output.xlsx"

# Define the keywords to search for (case-insensitive)
keywords = [
    'Core Activation',
    'Core Design', 
    'Mobile Terminology',
    'Mobile Design',
    'Mobile Integration',
    'Testing Layer',
    'Testing Offshore',
    'Carrier',
    'Barrier'
]

# Load the workbook using openpyxl to preserve formatting
wb = load_workbook(input_path)
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Get the maximum row with data
max_row = ws.max_row

# Process each row starting from row 2 (assuming row 1 is header)
for row_num in range(2, max_row + 1):
    # Get the value in column D for this row
    cell_d = ws[f'D{row_num}']
    cell_value = cell_d.value
    
    # Skip if cell is empty or None
    if cell_value is None:
        continue
    
    # Convert to string for text processing
    cell_text = str(cell_value).strip()
    
    # Skip if empty string
    if not cell_text:
        continue
    
    # Check if any keyword is found (case-insensitive, partial match)
    match_found = False
    cell_text_lower = cell_text.lower()
    
    for keyword in keywords:
        if keyword.lower() in cell_text_lower:
            match_found = True
            break
    
    # Set the value in column F based on match result
    cell_f = ws[f'F{row_num}']
    if match_found:
        cell_f.value = "Billing PO"
    else:
        # Leave blank (set to None if there was a value before)
        cell_f.value = None

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save(output_path)

print("Spreadsheet processing completed successfully!")

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Load the spreadsheet
file_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/194-19/1_194-19_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_194-19_output.xlsx"

# Read both sheets
sheet1_df = pd.read_excel(file_path, sheet_name='Sheet1')
sheet2_df = pd.read_excel(file_path, sheet_name='Sheet2')

# Print column names to debug
print("Sheet1 columns:", sheet1_df.columns.tolist())
print("Sheet2 columns:", sheet2_df.columns.tolist())
print("Sheet2 first few rows:")
print(sheet2_df.head())

# Load workbook for writing
wb = load_workbook(file_path)
ws1 = wb['Sheet1']

# Create a mapping from Sheet2 for easy lookup
# The actual column names seem to be different, let me use column positions instead
sheet2_lookup = {}

for idx, row in sheet2_df.iterrows():
    # Based on the sample data, the columns appear to be:
    # Column D (index 3): Track/Meet name
    # Column H (index 7): Race#
    # Column I (index 8): Top
    # Columns J, K, L, M (indices 9, 10, 11, 12): Results (1, 2, 3, 4)
    
    meet_name = row.iloc[3]  # Track column
    race_num = row.iloc[7]   # Race# column
    top_value = row.iloc[8]  # Top column
    
    # Get results from the last 4 columns (which should be the 1,2,3,4 columns)
    results = []
    for col_idx in range(len(row) - 4, len(row)):  # Last 4 columns
        if col_idx < len(row):
            results.append(row.iloc[col_idx])
        else:
            results.append(None)
    
    key = (meet_name, race_num)
    if key not in sheet2_lookup:
        sheet2_lookup[key] = []
    
    sheet2_lookup[key].append({
        'top': top_value,
        'results': results
    })

# Clear columns H and I in the specified range
for row_num in range(1, 371):
    ws1[f'H{row_num}'] = None
    ws1[f'I{row_num}'] = None

# Process each row in Sheet1
for idx, row in sheet1_df.iterrows():
    meet_name = row['Meet']
    race_num = row['Race#']
    excel_row = idx + 2  # Excel rows are 1-indexed, and we have headers
    
    key = (meet_name, race_num)
    
    if key in sheet2_lookup:
        match_data = sheet2_lookup[key][0]
        
        # Fill column H with Top value
        ws1[f'H{excel_row}'] = match_data['top']
        
        # For column I, we need to match based on the Tab number or ranking
        # Let's use the row's Tab value to determine which result to use
        tab_value = row.get('Tab', None)
        rank_value = row.get('Rank', None)
        
        # If we have a rank, use it to index into results (1-indexed)
        if pd.notna(rank_value) and rank_value > 0:
            result_idx = int(rank_value) - 1
            if result_idx < len(match_data['results']):
                result_value = match_data['results'][result_idx]
                if pd.notna(result_value):
                    ws1[f'I{excel_row}'] = result_value
        else:
            # If no rank, use the first available result
            for result in match_data['results']:
                if pd.notna(result):
                    ws1[f'I{excel_row}'] = result
                    break

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save(output_path)
wb.close()

print("Spreadsheet processing completed successfully!")

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Load the spreadsheet
wb = load_workbook('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/203-15/1_203-15_init.xlsx')

# Read the main sheet data
main_sheet = wb['Main']
main_data = []
for row in main_sheet.iter_rows(min_row=2, values_only=True):
    if row[0] is not None:  # Skip empty rows
        main_data.append(row)

# Convert to DataFrame for easier manipulation
df = pd.DataFrame(main_data, columns=[
    'staff no.', 'Department', 'Location', 'Name', 'Position', 'Gender', 'Nationality', 'Description', 'Amount', 'Currency'
])

# Group by staff member and pivot the allowance data
staff_columns = ['staff no.', 'Department', 'Location', 'Name', 'Position', 'Gender', 'Nationality']
staff_data = df[staff_columns].drop_duplicates().reset_index(drop=True)

# Create pivot table for allowances
allowance_pivot = df.pivot_table(
    index=['staff no.', 'Department', 'Location', 'Name', 'Position', 'Gender', 'Nationality'],
    columns='Description',
    values='Amount',
    fill_value=0
).reset_index()

# Merge staff data with allowance data
output_data = allowance_pivot

# Ensure we have all expected allowance columns
expected_allowances = ['Basic', 'Housing Allowance', 'Transport Allowances', 'Other Allowances', 'Social Allowances']
for allowance in expected_allowances:
    if allowance not in output_data.columns:
        output_data[allowance] = 0

# Calculate total for each row
allowance_cols = [col for col in output_data.columns if col not in staff_columns]
output_data['Total'] = output_data[allowance_cols].sum(axis=1)

# Create or get the Output Required sheet
if 'Output Required' in wb.sheetnames:
    output_sheet = wb['Output Required']
    # Clear existing data
    output_sheet.delete_rows(1, output_sheet.max_row)
else:
    output_sheet = wb.create_sheet('Output Required')

# Write headers (A1:G1 from main sheet + allowance headers + Total)
headers = ['staff no.', 'Department', 'Location', 'Name', 'Position', 'Gender', 'Nationality']
headers.extend(['Basic', 'Housing Allowance', 'Transport Allowances', 'Other Allowances', 'Social Allowances', 'Total'])

for col_idx, header in enumerate(headers, 1):
    output_sheet.cell(row=1, column=col_idx, value=header)

# Write data rows
for row_idx, (_, row_data) in enumerate(output_data.iterrows(), 2):
    # Write staff information (columns A-G)
    for col_idx, col_name in enumerate(staff_columns, 1):
        output_sheet.cell(row=row_idx, column=col_idx, value=row_data[col_name])
    
    # Write allowance amounts (columns H-L)
    col_idx = 8  # Column H
    for allowance in expected_allowances:
        if allowance in row_data:
            output_sheet.cell(row=row_idx, column=col_idx, value=row_data[allowance])
        else:
            output_sheet.cell(row=row_idx, column=col_idx, value=0)
        col_idx += 1
    
    # Write total in column M
    output_sheet.cell(row=row_idx, column=13, value=row_data['Total'])

# Save the workbook
wb.save('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_203-15_output.xlsx')

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook
from openpyxl.styles import Font, Fill, Border, Alignment
import numpy as np

# Load the workbook
wb = load_workbook('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/208-20/1_208-20_init.xlsx')
ws = wb['Q']

# First, let's extract the actual data to understand the structure
# Read the data to see what we're working with
data = []
for row in range(1, 38):  # A1:F37 range
    row_data = []
    for col in range(1, 7):  # Columns A-F
        cell = ws.cell(row=row, column=col)
        row_data.append(cell.value)
    data.append(row_data)

# Find where the actual data starts (after headers)
# Looking for the header row with 'R DAYS'
header_row = None
for i, row in enumerate(data):
    if row and len(row) > 4 and 'R DAYS' in str(row[4]):  # Check column E for 'R DAYS'
        header_row = i
        break

if header_row is not None:
    # Extract the actual data rows (excluding header)
    data_start_row = header_row + 1
    data_rows = []
    
    for i in range(data_start_row, len(data)):
        row = data[i]
        # Only include rows that have some data
        if any(cell is not None and str(cell).strip() != '' for cell in row):
            data_rows.append((i, row))
    
    # Sort by column E (index 4) - R DAYS column, from highest to lowest
    def sort_key(item):
        row_data = item[1]
        if len(row_data) > 4:
            r_days_value = row_data[4]  # Column E (R DAYS)
            if r_days_value is not None:
                try:
                    return float(r_days_value)
                except (ValueError, TypeError):
                    return float('-inf')
        return float('-inf')
    
    sorted_data = sorted(data_rows, key=sort_key, reverse=True)
    
    # Store the original cell data with their formatting
    original_cells = {}
    for orig_row_idx, _ in data_rows:
        row_cells = []
        for col in range(1, 7):  # Columns A-F
            cell = ws.cell(row=orig_row_idx + 1, column=col)  # +1 because ws is 1-indexed
            
            # Store cell properties that can be safely copied
            cell_data = {
                'value': cell.value,
                'number_format': cell.number_format,
                'font_name': cell.font.name,
                'font_size': cell.font.size,
                'font_bold': cell.font.bold,
                'font_italic': cell.font.italic,
                'font_color': cell.font.color,
                'fill_fgColor': cell.fill.fgColor,
                'fill_patternType': cell.fill.patternType,
                'alignment_horizontal': cell.alignment.horizontal,
                'alignment_vertical': cell.alignment.vertical
            }
            row_cells.append(cell_data)
        original_cells[orig_row_idx] = row_cells
    
    # Apply the sorted data back to the worksheet
    for new_idx, (orig_row_idx, _) in enumerate(sorted_data):
        target_row = data_start_row + 1 + new_idx  # +1 for 1-based indexing
        
        if orig_row_idx in original_cells:
            for col in range(6):  # Columns A-F (0-indexed)
                cell = ws.cell(row=target_row, column=col + 1)
                stored_cell = original_cells[orig_row_idx][col]
                
                # Set the value
                cell.value = stored_cell['value']
                cell.number_format = stored_cell['number_format']
                
                # Create new font object
                if stored_cell['font_name'] or stored_cell['font_size']:
                    cell.font = Font(
                        name=stored_cell['font_name'],
                        size=stored_cell['font_size'],
                        bold=stored_cell['font_bold'],
                        italic=stored_cell['font_italic'],
                        color=stored_cell['font_color']
                    )
                
                # Create new fill object if needed
                if stored_cell['fill_patternType'] and stored_cell['fill_patternType'] != 'none':
                    cell.fill = Fill(
                        patternType=stored_cell['fill_patternType'],
                        fgColor=stored_cell['fill_fgColor']
                    )
                
                # Create new alignment object
                if stored_cell['alignment_horizontal'] or stored_cell['alignment_vertical']:
                    cell.alignment = Alignment(
                        horizontal=stored_cell['alignment_horizontal'],
                        vertical=stored_cell['alignment_vertical']
                    )

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_208-20_output.xlsx')

helper_column = df['LIST'].copy()

# Remove rows where NAME is empty/NaN (skip empty cells and focus on NAME column)
source_data = source_data.dropna(subset=['NAME']).reset_index(drop=True)

# Convert REF column to string to handle mixed data types, then handle sorting
source_data['REF'] = source_data['REF'].astype(str)

# Remove duplicates based on identical entries in both column B (NAME) and C (REF)
source_data = source_data.drop_duplicates(subset=['NAME', 'REF'], keep='first').reset_index(drop=True)

# Get the helper list (removing NaN values)

    sorted_data = source_data.sort_values(['NAME', 'REF'], ascending=[True, True]).reset_index(drop=True)

    sorted_rows = []

    for helper_name in helper_list:

If the helper column J is empty, only then sort alphabetically A–Z and still skip empty cells, headers, and duplicate items. import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Load the spreadsheet
spreadsheet_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/227-40/1_227-40_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_227-40_output.xlsx"

# Load workbook
wb = load_workbook(spreadsheet_path)
ws = wb['Sheet1']

# Read the data from the first few rows
data = []
for row in range(1, 5):  # Rows 1-4 (including header)
    row_data = []
    for col in range(1, 6):  # Columns A-E
        cell_value = ws.cell(row=row, column=col).value
        row_data.append(cell_value if cell_value is not None else "")
    data.append(row_data)

print("Original data:")
for i, row in enumerate(data):
    print(f"Row {i+1}: {row}")

# Function to remove duplicates within a row while preserving order
def remove_row_duplicates(row_data):
    result = []
    seen = set()
    
    for item in row_data:
        # Convert to string and strip whitespace for comparison
        item_str = str(item).strip() if item is not None else ""
        
        # If it's empty or we haven't seen this item before, add it
        if item_str == "" or item_str not in seen:
            result.append(item)
            if item_str != "":
                seen.add(item_str)
        else:
            # If it's a duplicate, replace with empty string
            result.append("")
    
    return result

# Process the data to remove duplicates within each row
processed_data = []
for row in data:
    processed_row = remove_row_duplicates(row)
    processed_data.append(processed_row)

print("\nProcessed data (duplicates removed):")
for i, row in enumerate(processed_data):
    print(f"Row {i+1}: {row}")

# Write the processed data to cells A14:E17
start_row = 14
for i, row in enumerate(processed_data):
    for j, value in enumerate(row):
        cell = ws.cell(row=start_row + i, column=j + 1)
        cell.value = value if value != "" else None

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save(output_path)
print(f"\nResults saved to: {output_path}")
print("Data has been written to range A14:E17")

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Read the Excel file
spreadsheet_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/23-24/1_23-24_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_23-24_output.xlsx"

# Load the workbook and worksheet
wb = load_workbook(spreadsheet_path)
ws = wb.active

# Read data into pandas for easier manipulation
df = pd.read_excel(spreadsheet_path, header=0)

# Get the words from column I (excluding NaN values)
column_i_words = set()
for value in df.iloc[:, 8]:  # Column I is index 8
    if pd.notna(value) and isinstance(value, str):
        column_i_words.add(value.strip().upper())

# Create a list to track rows to keep
rows_to_keep = []

# Check each row in the dataframe
for index, row in df.iterrows():
    column_a_value = row.iloc[0]  # Column A is index 0
    
    # Check if column A value matches any word in column I
    if pd.notna(column_a_value) and isinstance(column_a_value, str):
        if column_a_value.strip().upper() not in column_i_words:
            rows_to_keep.append(index)
    else:
        rows_to_keep.append(index)

# Filter the dataframe to keep only non-matching rows
filtered_df = df.iloc[rows_to_keep].copy()

# Create a new workbook with the filtered data
from openpyxl import Workbook
new_wb = Workbook()
new_ws = new_wb.active
new_ws.title = "Sheet1"

# Write the header row
header_row = df.columns.tolist()
for col_idx, header in enumerate(header_row, 1):
    new_ws.cell(row=1, column=col_idx, value=header)

# Write the filtered data
for row_idx, (_, row_data) in enumerate(filtered_df.iterrows(), 2):
    for col_idx in range(5):  # Only columns A through E (0-4)
        if col_idx < len(row_data):
            value = row_data.iloc[col_idx]
            if pd.notna(value):
                new_ws.cell(row=row_idx, column=col_idx + 1, value=value)

# Also copy the remaining columns (F, G, H, I, etc.) for completeness
for row_idx, (_, row_data) in enumerate(filtered_df.iterrows(), 2):
    for col_idx in range(5, len(row_data)):  # Columns F onwards
        value = row_data.iloc[col_idx]
        if pd.notna(value):
            new_ws.cell(row=row_idx, column=col_idx + 1, value=value)

# Save the modified workbook
new_wb.save(output_path)

print(f"Successfully processed the spreadsheet. Removed rows where column A matched words in column I.")
print(f"Output saved to: {output_path}")

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import re

# Load the spreadsheet
spreadsheet_path = '/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/230-16/1_230-16_init.xlsx'
output_path = '/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_230-16_output.xlsx'

# Load the workbook
wb = load_workbook(spreadsheet_path)
ws = wb['Before']

# Process each cell in column A (from A1 to A12 as specified in answer_position)
for row in range(1, 13):  # A1 to A12
    cell_value = ws[f'A{row}'].value
    
    if cell_value and isinstance(cell_value, str):
        # Find the first letter in the string
        first_letter_match = re.search(r'[a-zA-Z]', cell_value)
        
        if first_letter_match:
            first_letter_pos = first_letter_match.start()
            
            # Split the string: part before first letter stays in A, part from first letter goes to B
            part_before = cell_value[:first_letter_pos].strip()
            part_from_letter = cell_value[first_letter_pos:].strip()
            
            # Update column A with the part before the first letter
            ws[f'A{row}'].value = part_before if part_before else None
            
            # Put the substring starting from first letter into column B
            ws[f'B{row}'].value = part_from_letter
        else:
            # If no letter found, keep original in A and put nothing in B
            ws[f'B{row}'].value = None
    else:
        # If cell is empty or not a string, put nothing in B
        ws[f'B{row}'].value = None

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save(output_path)

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook

# Load the workbook
wb = load_workbook('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/236-22/1_236-22_init.xlsx')

# Get all sheet names
sheet_names = wb.sheetnames

# Process each sheet (starting from 'BR1' onwards, but since we only have Sheet1, we'll process it)
for sheet_name in sheet_names:
    if sheet_name == 'Sheet1' or sheet_name.startswith('BR'):
        ws = wb[sheet_name]
        
        # Convert to pandas DataFrame to work with the data more easily
        data = []
        for row in ws.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=16, min_col=1, max_col=5, values_only=True):
            data.append(list(row))
        
        # Create DataFrame
        df = pd.DataFrame(data)
        
        # Find rows to delete (working backwards to avoid index issues)
        rows_to_delete = []
        
        for i in range(len(df) - 1):  # -1 because we check i+1
            # Check if current row has "Line No" in column A (index 0)
            if df.iloc[i, 0] == "Line No":
                # Check if the row below is blank in column A
                if pd.isna(df.iloc[i + 1, 0]) or df.iloc[i + 1, 0] == "":
                    # Mark both rows for deletion
                    rows_to_delete.extend([i, i + 1])
        
        # Remove duplicates and sort in descending order
        rows_to_delete = sorted(list(set(rows_to_delete)), reverse=True)
        
        # Delete rows from the worksheet (working backwards)
        for row_idx in rows_to_delete:
            ws.delete_rows(row_idx + 1)  # +1 because openpyxl uses 1-based indexing
        
        # Clear any remaining cells beyond our data range to ensure clean output
        for row in ws.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=16, min_col=1, max_col=5):
            for cell in row:
                if cell.row > (16 - len(rows_to_delete)):
                    cell.value = None

# Save the modified workbook
wb.save('/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_236-22_output.xlsx')

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import numpy as np

# Load the Excel file
file_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/247-24/1_247-24_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_247-24_output.xlsx"

# Load both sheets
main_df = pd.read_excel(file_path, sheet_name='Main')
lookup_df = pd.read_excel(file_path, sheet_name='Lookup')

print("Original Main sheet shape:", main_df.shape)
print("Original Main sheet columns:", main_df.columns.tolist())

# Task 1: Delete rows where Company name is 'Motorcycle'
main_df = main_df[main_df['Company'] != 'Motorcycle']
print("After deleting Motorcycle rows:", main_df.shape)

# Task 2: Delete rows where Company is 'Ahmed Sons' AND Location is 'Canada'
main_df = main_df[~((main_df['Company'] == 'Ahmed Sons') & (main_df['Location'] == 'Canada'))]
print("After deleting Ahmed Sons + Canada rows:", main_df.shape)

# Task 3: Change Bill Rate of Resource to 180 for National TV + India
main_df.loc[(main_df['Company'] == 'National TV') & (main_df['Location'] == 'India'), 'Bill Rate of Resource'] = 180
print("Updated Bill Rate for National TV + India rows")

# Task 4: Insert two new rows after each 'Ahmed Sons' row
# We need to do this carefully to maintain the structure
new_rows = []
for index, row in main_df.iterrows():
    new_rows.append(row)
    if row['Company'] == 'Ahmed Sons':
        # Insert two empty rows with same structure
        empty_row1 = row.copy()
        empty_row2 = row.copy()
        # Clear the data in the empty rows (keep structure but clear values)
        for col in empty_row1.index:
            if col not in ['Company', 'Location']:  # Keep some identifying info or clear all
                empty_row1[col] = np.nan
                empty_row2[col] = np.nan
        new_rows.append(empty_row1)
        new_rows.append(empty_row2)

# Create new dataframe from the expanded rows
main_df = pd.DataFrame(new_rows).reset_index(drop=True)
print("After inserting rows after Ahmed Sons:", main_df.shape)

# Task 5: Perform VLOOKUP to populate Column M (Lookup For Working Weeks) 
# with Working Weeks from Lookup sheet, matching on Employee ID
main_df = main_df.merge(lookup_df, left_on='Employee ID', right_on='Employee ID', how='left')

# If the merge created a new column, we need to update the correct column
if 'Working Weeks' in main_df.columns:
    main_df['Lookup For Working Weeks'] = main_df['Working Weeks']
    main_df = main_df.drop('Working Weeks', axis=1)

print("After VLOOKUP operation:", main_df.shape)
print("Final columns:", main_df.columns.tolist())

# Ensure we only keep data within the specified range (A2:M70 equivalent)
# Since we're working with pandas, we'll limit rows to fit the range
max_rows = 69  # A2:M70 means 69 data rows (excluding header)
if len(main_df) > max_rows:
    main_df = main_df.head(max_rows)

# Clean up any remaining data in deleted rows by ensuring NaN values are properly handled
main_df = main_df.replace('', np.nan)

# Save the results
with pd.ExcelWriter(output_path, engine='openpyxl') as writer:
    main_df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name='Main', index=False)
    lookup_df.to_excel(writer, sheet_name='Lookup', index=False)

print(f"File saved to: {output_path}")
print("Final Main sheet shape:", main_df.shape)

# Verify the results
print("\nVerification:")
print("Motorcycle rows remaining:", len(main_df[main_df['Company'] == 'Motorcycle']))
print("Ahmed Sons + Canada rows remaining:", len(main_df[(main_df['Company'] == 'Ahmed Sons') & (main_df['Location'] == 'Canada')]))
print("National TV + India rows with Bill Rate 180:", len(main_df[(main_df['Company'] == 'National TV') & (main_df['Location'] == 'India') & (main_df['Bill Rate of Resource'] == 180)]))
print("Rows with populated Working Weeks:", len(main_df[main_df['Lookup For Working Weeks'].notna()]))

import pandas as pd
import openpyxl
from openpyxl.utils.dataframe import dataframe_to_rows

# Load the Excel file
file_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/spreadsheet/250-20/1_250-20_init.xlsx"
output_path = "/Users/thomascahill/Documents/servalsheets 2/benchmark/spreadsheetbench/data/spreadsheetbench_verified_400/outputs/single_claude-sonnet-4-20250514/1_250-20_output.xlsx"

# Read the RNM sheet
df = pd.read_excel(file_path, sheet_name='RNM')

# Print the original data to understand the structure
print("Original data:")
print(df.head(10))
print(f"Shape: {df.shape}")
print(f"Columns: {list(df.columns)}")

# Group by columns B (NUMBER) and C (LINE NO) and sum column J (MATCHED_QTY)
# We'll keep the first row of each group and update the MATCHED_QTY with the sum
grouped = df.groupby(['NUMBER', 'LINE NO']).agg({
    'DATE': 'first',
    'NUMBER': 'first', 
    'LINE NO': 'first',
    'NAME': 'first',
    'EMAIL ADDRESS': 'first',
    'CAT NO': 'first',
    'EXT CAT ID': 'first',
    'COLOR': 'first',
    'QTY': 'first',
    'MATCHED_QTY': 'sum',  # Sum the MATCHED_QTY values
    'PCR_USR_FLD_04': 'first'
}).reset_index(drop=True)

# Reorder columns to match original
result_df = grouped[df.columns]

print("\nProcessed data:")
print(result_df.head(10))
print(f"Shape after processing: {result_df.shape}")

# Create a new workbook and add the processed data
wb = openpyxl.Workbook()
ws = wb.active
ws.title = 'RNM'

# Add the data to the worksheet
for r in dataframe_to_rows(result_df, index=False, header=True):
    ws.append(r)

# Save the workbook
wb.save(output_path)

print(f"\nFile saved to: {output_path}")
